summary: Create a comprehensive architecture document addressing the design of a system with specified components, interfaces, risks, and trade-offs. The document will include a problem summary, proposed approach, and an acceptance checklist.

artifacts.doc_markdown:
## Problem Summary
- Define the system's core functionality and user needs.

## Proposed Approach
- Outline the architecture with key components and their interactions.

## Architecture Diagram
```mermaid
graph TD;
    A[Component A] --> B[Component B];
    A --> C[Component C];
    B --> D[Component D];
```  

## Components
- Component A: Handles user authentication.
- Component B: Manages data processing.
- Component C: Provides API endpoints.
- Component D: Stores data in a database.

## Interfaces
- Component A <-> Component B: REST API for data exchange.
- Component C <-> Component D: SQL queries for data retrieval.

## Risks & Trade-offs
- Risk of data inconsistency if components fail to communicate.
- Trade-off between performance and complexity in data processing.

## Acceptance Checklist
- All components are documented.
- Interfaces are clearly defined.
- Risks have been identified and mitigations proposed.

components.name: Authentication Service
components.responsibility: Handles user login and token generation

components.name: Data Processing Service
components.responsibility: Processes incoming data and applies business logic

interfaces.description: Routes API calls to processing service

tradeoffs: Using a microservices architecture increases complexity but allows for independent scaling.
Choosing PostgreSQL provides strong consistency but may limit performance under heavy load.

rubric_self_check.overall_notes: All required sections are covered, with clear components, interfaces, and risks identified.
